question: What is the definition of the abbreviation CNV?

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates at full power using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps. Its small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design features passive safety systems, dedicated emergency cooling, and decay heat removal, all housed in a steel containment partially immersed in water for effective passive heat removal. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its components are designed for modular fabrication and enhanced safety.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and other instrumentation and accessory devices, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0023.title: Containment Systems
nuscale_ch01_0023.summary: The NuScale containment vessel (CNV) is kept under vacuum during normal operation, which reduces moisture and corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits oxygen for safety. In the event of an emergency, passive cooling rapidly reduces pressure and temperature, and the design’s reliance on passive safety systems and small fuel inventory results in improved safety and a lower core damage frequency compared to traditional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It describes the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system operation and status indication, as well as notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements.
